skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: An agent’s total earnings consist of a base salary of x dollars per year, plus commission earnings of 8% of the total sales the agent makes during the year. This year, the agent has a goal for the total earnings to be at least 4 times and at most 5 times the base salary. Which of the following inequalities represents all possible values of total sales s, in dollars, the agent can make this year in order to meet that goal?
rationale: Choice C is correct. Total earnings = x + 0.08s. The goal: 4x ≤ x + 0.08s ≤ 5x. Subtract x: 3x ≤ 0.08s ≤ 4x. Divide by 0.08: 3/0.08 x ≤ s ≤ 4/0.08 x. Choice A and D conflate total sales with total earnings. Choice B uses the wrong multiples (2 and 3 instead of 3 and 4).
